name: bomet-ke
_comment: What this deployment SHOULD have. The profile discovers what it DOES have; this file
decides whether a gap is a bug (required -> FAIL) or legitimately N/A (absent -> SKIP).
Measured read-only against bomet on 2026-07-16. Bomet is a FLAT single tenant `ke`
(state_root = state_tenant_id = tenant_id = ke), hierarchy ADMIN, County/SubCounty/Ward.
`ke.bomet` also exists in the DB but is legacy from the original two-level bring-up that
was later collapsed; the suite targets `ke`, which is self-sufficient.

_rationale.workflow.pgr.actions.ESCALATE: ke's PGR businessService DOES define ESCALATE (2 transition rows), so #521 must actually run here — it is the deployment the feature was built for.
_rationale.mdms.rejectionReasons: Deliberate red. Confirmed absent: no schema definition and no data on any tenant. The repo carries a seed at local-setup/ansible/nairobi-mdms/mdms/data/ke/RAINMAKER-PGR/RejectionReasons.json that has never been loaded.
_rationale.personas.gro-with-department: Required, and currently the largest gap: bomet.env sets no GRO_USER so it defaults to ADMIN, which has NO HRMS employee record at ke -> DEPARTMENT_NOT_FOUND -> the lifecycle seed fails and cascades. NOTE: BOMET_ADMIN is NOT a valid answer — its HRMS record lives at ke.bomet, and HRMS does not fall back to a parent tenant. Viable actors at ke include HS_GRO (dept DEPT_1). Departments holding BOTH employees and complaint types at ke: MEDICAL_SVC, WATER_ENV, ADMIN_PUBLIC_SVC, DEPT_1, LANDS_HOUSING, DEPT_2, ENV.
_rationale.personas.ward-scoped-csr: BOMET_CSR_CHESOEN_* exists here, so the boundary-jurisdiction #496 regression is meaningful and must not silently skip.
_rationale.locales.multi: hi_IN, en_IN, fr_FR and pt_PT all carry substantial message counts, but the suite's completeness assertions are only calibrated for en_IN — optional until they are.
_rationale.tenant.citySubTenant: Flat `ke` deployment: there is no city sub-tenant, so the configurator city-search test is genuinely N/A.
